concerns.text: T023 implements the Reference-Validator Agent using a 'stratified random sample' but omits the mandatory 'deterministic seed-based subset selection' required by FR-014 and Constitution Principle II. This semantic weakening breaks reproducibility (Constitution I) and violates the specific constraint in FR-014.

T014 is scoped to 'calculate vulnerability_count... per sample' but FR-003 mandates the system 'MUST execute static analysis tools... on all generated and human-written code samples'. The task list lacks a distinct task to actually run the Bandit execution loop and generate the vulnerability reports; T014 assumes the reports exist or conflates execution with calculation, creating a gap in the FR-003 deliverable.

T012 describes iterating 'HumanEval/MBPP tasks' without explicitly enforcing the 'random set of tasks' constraint mandated by FR-002. Implementing this task literally could result in a full-benchmark run (scope creep) rather than the specified random subset, violating the experimental design constraint.

T037 verifies reproducibility by checking 'absolute difference ≤1e-6' but fails to specify WHICH metric (e.g., p-value, IRR, vulnerability count) is being compared. SC-005 requires measuring the reproducibility of 'statistical outputs', and without specifying the metric, the verification step is ambiguous and potentially untestable against the spec.
